goal:
Fix 4 high-severity audit findings. DO NOT add new features, only fix the issues described:

1. SUPERVISOR prompt injection: In internal/orchestrator/service.go, wherever worker/system summaries or block reasons are written to job.LeaderContextSummary, strip any text that starts with "[SUPERVISOR]" from the untrusted input. Add a helper sanitizeLeaderContext(text string) string that removes lines starting with "[SUPERVISOR]". Apply it in runWorkerStep and runSystemStep when setting LeaderContextSummary.

2. Workspace validation strengthening: In both internal/orchestrator/service.go validateWorkspaceDir and internal/mcp/server.go validateWorkspaceDir, add: (a) require absolute path using filepath.IsAbs, (b) resolve symlinks with filepath.EvalSymlinks before stat check. Unify the two copies into one shared function if possible (put in a new file internal/orchestrator/workspace.go and import from mcp).

3. Steer authorization: In internal/orchestrator/service.go Steer method, check that job.Status is "running" or "waiting_leader" or "waiting_worker" before accepting the directive. Return error for done/failed/cancelled jobs.

4. Steer directive preservation: In internal/orchestrator/service.go, instead of writing to LeaderContextSummary directly, add a new field SupervisorDirective string to domain.Job (in internal/domain/types.go). Steer writes to this field. In internal/provider/protocol.go buildLeaderPrompt, if job.SupervisorDirective is non-empty, include it as a separate section BEFORE the job state. After the leader processes it (when runLoop gets the next leader response), clear the field.

Run go build ./... and go test ./... to verify.

verification_contract.goal: Verify that the four audit findings are fixed with no scope expansion and that the codebase still builds and tests cleanly.
scope: Leader-context sanitization for untrusted worker/system input.
Shared workspace validation with absolute-path and symlink resolution checks.
Steer authorization restricted to active job states.
Supervisor directive preservation, prompt placement, and one-time clearing.
Regression coverage and repository-wide build/test verification.
required_checks: Confirm `sanitizeLeaderContext` exists and is used at the `LeaderContextSummary` assignment sites fed by worker/system outputs or block reasons.
Confirm shared workspace validation enforces `filepath.IsAbs` and calls `filepath.EvalSymlinks` before directory existence/type checks.
Confirm `Steer` rejects non-active statuses and no longer stores directives in `LeaderContextSummary`.
Confirm `domain.Job.SupervisorDirective` is rendered as a distinct leader-prompt section before job state and is cleared after the subsequent leader response is processed.
Confirm any new or updated tests cover the changed behaviors and repository-wide build/test commands pass.
disallowed_actions: Do not add new features or broaden scope beyond the four findings.
Do not bypass evaluator-gate or alter approval/harness semantics.
Do not keep duplicated workspace validators if a cycle-free shared helper is possible.
Do not let untrusted `[SUPERVISOR]` lines reach `LeaderContextSummary`.
Do not store supervisor directives in `LeaderContextSummary` after the fix.
Do not introduce Windows-only core logic.
verification_contract.notes: Prefer a minimal patch set. If importing orchestrator from MCP would create a package cycle, move the shared validator to the smallest cycle-free internal package while preserving the same behavior and scope.

summary: The required audit fixes were implemented in scope, focused tests were added or updated for each behavior, and verification was reported complete with `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` passing. Sprint step coverage is satisfied, so this is ready for evaluator-gated completion.
leader_context_summary: The job state includes a succeeded implement step that directly covers all four audit fixes and reports successful repository-wide verification with `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`.

steps.task_text: Implement the 4 audit fixes in a minimal patch set and verify with full build/test. Scope: (1) In `internal/orchestrator/service.go`, add `sanitizeLeaderContext(text string) string` that removes any line starting with `[SUPERVISOR]`, and apply it anywhere untrusted worker/system summaries or block reasons are assigned to `job.LeaderContextSummary` in `runWorkerStep` and `runSystemStep`. (2) Strengthen workspace validation by requiring absolute paths with `filepath.IsAbs` and resolving symlinks with `filepath.EvalSymlinks` before stat/type checks; unify the duplicated validators from `internal/orchestrator/service.go` and `internal/mcp/server.go` into one shared helper if cycle-free, preferably `internal/orchestrator/workspace.go`, otherwise the smallest shared internal package. (3) In `internal/orchestrator/service.go` `Steer`, reject directives unless `job.Status` is `running`, `waiting_leader`, or `waiting_worker`; return an error for done/failed/cancelled states. (4) Preserve supervisor directives separately: add `SupervisorDirective string` to `internal/domain/types.go`; make `Steer` write there instead of `LeaderContextSummary`; in `internal/provider/protocol.go` `buildLeaderPrompt`, render `SupervisorDirective` as its own section before the job state; after the subsequent leader response is processed in the run loop, clear `job.SupervisorDirective`. Add or update focused tests for these behaviors without expanding scope. Update docs only if directly required by the changed behavior. Then run `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` and report changed files, test coverage added, and verification results.
steps.summary: Implemented the 4 audit fixes in a minimal patch set. Changed files: internal/domain/types.go, internal/orchestrator/workspace.go, internal/orchestrator/service.go, internal/mcp/server.go, internal/provider/protocol.go, internal/orchestrator/service_test.go, internal/mcp/server_test.go, internal/provider/provider_test.go. Key fixes: added Job.SupervisorDirective and moved Steer to use it; restricted Steer to running/waiting_leader/waiting_worker; cleared SupervisorDirective after the next successful leader turn is received; added sanitizeLeaderContext and applied it to worker/system leader-context writes plus blocked-reason persistence; centralized workspace validation in orchestrator.ValidateWorkspaceDir with absolute-path enforcement and EvalSymlinks before stat checks; MCP now uses the shared validator; leader prompt renders a separate Supervisor directive section before job state and excludes the directive from the serialized job payload. Test coverage added/updated for absolute-path validation, symlink validation, steer persistence/rejection, one-time directive clearing, worker leader-context sanitization, MCP relative-workspace rejection, and leader-prompt supervisor section placement. Commands run: gofmt -w on modified files; go test ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/mcp ./internal/provider -run <focused tests>; go build ./...; go test ./.... Errors encountered and resolved during implementation: initial targeted tests exposed Windows EvalSymlinks returning "Access is denied" for non-symlink temp directories, so the shared validator now falls back to the cleaned path only after attempting EvalSymlinks and confirming the existing path is not itself a symlink; targeted test fixtures also needed MaxSteps/step-state adjustments and a valid blocked_reason for blocked worker schema validation. Final verification: go build ./... passed, go test ./... passed.

Final verification: go build ./... passed, go test ./... passed.
The job state includes a succeeded implement step that directly covers all four audit fixes and reports successful repository-wide verification with `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`.

The required audit fixes were implemented in scope, focused tests were added or updated for each behavior, and verification was reported complete with `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` passing. Sprint step coverage is satisfied, so this is ready for evaluator-gated completion.
